original_registry_entry.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
original_registry_entry.population: Children, young people, and adults with community- or hospital-acquired pneumonia, subject to recommendation-specific setting and age qualifiers.
original_registry_entry.clinical_setting: First contact, primary care, hospital, community treatment, reassessment, and post-discharge follow-up.
original_registry_entry.applicability_note: Exact for selected pneumonia recovery, reassessment, observations, and post-inpatient chest-radiograph fields; international guidance requires UAE clinician review and the legacy workflow does not enforce every age or discharge qualifier.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.2 and 1.6.7 plus terms — clinical observations and signs
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports clinician-recorded temperature, respiratory rate, oxygen saturation, mental status, eating or hydration context, and focal chest examination findings without asserting severity or diagnosis.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.10.1–1.10.4 — recovery and when to seek further advice
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports review of fever, cough, sputum, breathlessness, chest pain, fatigue or functional recovery, worsening or non-improving symptoms, systemic illness, and clinician-documented safety advice, with adult and child qualifiers preserved.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.11.1–1.11.8 — reassessment
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports reassessment when symptoms or signs do not improve or worsen, clinician-led referral, microbiology where specified, and CRP or procalcitonin review in the qualified hospital treatment-failure context.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.12.1–1.12.3 — follow-up chest X-rays
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports no routine post-inpatient follow-up radiograph, consideration at 6 weeks for smoking, age over 50, persistent or deteriorating symptoms, or unexplained weight loss, and shared decision-making using recent imaging, comorbidity, frailty, prognosis, treatment options, and preferences.

title: Pneumonia: diagnosis and management
publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
population: Children, young people, and adults with community- or hospital-acquired pneumonia, subject to recommendation-specific setting and age qualifiers.
intended_setting: First contact, primary care, hospital, community treatment, reassessment, and post-discharge follow-up.
